scope: Live password-reset request through KISS PM auth routes with REG.RU SMTP, Cloudflare Email Routing delivery to Gmail, token extraction from delivered email, confirm/readback, and repeat-token behavior.
secretsPolicy: Raw reset token, passwords, SMTP password, and API keys are intentionally omitted. Token was extracted from Gmail in-memory only and not written to disk.

harness.dataSource: isolated auth-capable in-memory dataSource injected into createApp; product auth routes and SMTP provider are real
harness.limitation: This is not the full production Postgres stack; previous browser mechanics were already covered, and this run closes the external delivery/extraction gap.

mailReadback.providerPath: REG.RU SMTP -> Cloudflare Email Routing -> Gmail
mailReadback.subject: KISS PM password reset

passCriteria: Registered a fresh auth user with a unique @kisspm.app address.
POST /api/auth/password-reset/request returned 202 with delivery=email and created one reset token.
Gmail search found the delivered KISS PM password reset email for the unique recipient.
Extracted a 64-character reset token from the delivered email body, not from server memory.
POST /api/auth/password-reset/confirm with the delivered token returned 200 {status:ok}.
New password login returned 200 and Set-Cookie.
Repeating the same token returned 400 reset_token_used.
Original reset email shows SPF PASS, DKIM PASS, and DMARC PASS.
matrixImpact: Closes RISK-AUTH-EMAIL-RESET-HAPPY-PATH for live SMTP/email-client delivery and token extraction. Remaining risk zones are full browser traversal breadth and live media provider behavior.
